programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: How an Arizona woman’s house became a gateway for North Korea’s IT operatives, an underground laptop farm that infiltrated US companies and pumped millions to the regime.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: Rainbow Mountain offers tourists a picture-perfect view that has flooded social media feeds; behind this viral beauty spot is the story of a community at war, cries for help ignored and murder.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: Alleged scammers in the U.K. are accused of defrauding would-be migrant health care workers out of tens of thousands of pounds; some victims have turned to a vigilante scammer hunter for help.

programme.title: Power Players
programme.desc: Jason Kelly follows Pierre Gasly from Alpine Headquarters to a Paris photo shoot to get a firsthand look at how the business of being an F1 driver goes far beyond the track.

programme.title: Power Players
programme.desc: Jason Kelly heads to Maranello, Italy, to speak with Ferrari's leadership about the coming regulatory changes for Formula 1 and how Ferrari's racing technology informs the road cars of today.

programme.title: Power Players
programme.desc: Jason Kelly joins Miami Grand Prix Managing Partner Tom Garfinkel as he tackles the complexities of putting on the race and F1 CEO Stefano Domenicali reveals why it is key to F1's future growth.

programme.title: Power Players
programme.desc: Jason Kelly visits the UK's Motorsport Valley, where Williams Team Principal James Volwes and F1 Academy drivers' champion Abbi Pulling show how advanced simulation is shaping the future of Formula 1.

programme.title: Power Players
programme.desc: Major League Baseball Hall of Famer Cal Ripken Jr. and his brother Bill have turned youth sports into big business.

programme.title: Power Players
programme.desc: Toronto Raptors President Masai Ujiri leads the only non-US team in the National Basketball Association, and he's spearheading an effort to take the game global.

programme.title: Posthuman
programme.desc: Emily Chang meets some of the world's most advanced, and most intelligent robots and questions whether the creation of a synthetic species with human-like intelligence improves the human experience or ends it.

programme.title: Posthuman
programme.desc: Emily Chang explores the future of connections with the machines and the technologies powering a growing market of relationships-on-demand.

programme.title: Posthuman
programme.desc: From brain implants that allow paralyzed patients to communicate to the wearable devices enhancing human capabilities, brain-computer interfaces could change the way people use their minds forever.

programme.title: Posthuman
programme.desc: From birth to death, tech is stretching the boundaries of biology; exploring the discoveries that could transform reproduction, healthcare and how people die.

programme.title: Leaders with Francine Lacqua: Alexander Stubb, President of Finland
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua visits Finland, where the threat from neighboring Russia looms large; she speaks to President Alexander Stubb about Finnish resilience and how his country is preparing for an uncertain future.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua meets Ray Dalio on OceanX in the port of Nice for the UN Ocean Conference. They speak about radical transparency, building and leaving one of the largest hedge funds and what's next.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: Francine Lacqua talks with Franklin Templeton CEO Jenny Johnson about how to steer a $1.6 trillion business amid the rise of AI, shifting politics and booming private markets.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: Former U.K. Prime Minister Theresa May speaks with Francine Lacqua about Vladimir Putin and her approach to leadership in a world seeing a rise in strongmen.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: The anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas.

programme.title: LEADERS With Francine Lacqua
programme.desc: What drives the world's most influential minds? In Leaders with Francine Lacqua, we sit down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas.

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Humanoid robots that use AI are going mainstream, but they're far from proven. With billions of dollars invested, can they deliver real-world value, or will they fall short of the hype?

programme.title: Bloomberg Primer
programme.desc: Chinese companies dominate the EV battery supply chain and are expanding rapidly outside their home market. Meanwhile, competitors are looking to new chemistries to leapfrog the leading tech.

programme.title: The Hidden Cost of South Sudan's Oil Boom
programme.desc: South Sudan relies on oil for almost all of its revenue. But some communities living near the oil fields allege polluted water is causing birth defects and miscarriages, raising questions over health and responsibility.

programme.title: A Dangerous Corner of the Global Shipping Industry
programme.desc: The sinking of the Gulf Livestock 1 was the worst disaster in the history of the live-export trade, an industry made up of about 150 ships with a market value totaling $20 billion to $30 billion.

programme.title: How Chinese Brands Are Going Global
programme.desc: From Pop Mart's Labubus to Shein, Temu and Luckin, Chinese brands have become global consumer favorites and cultural ambassadors. With much of the credit going to TikTok, shoppers are hungry for more.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: How an Arizona woman’s house became a gateway for North Korea’s IT operatives, an underground laptop farm that infiltrated US companies and pumped millions to the regime.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: Gold built South Africa, but mining kills some who dig for it; in Stilfontein, a disaster that trapped miners underground reveals how a quest for gold became a story of hunger, survival and death.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission is supposed to root out graft; instead, some of its members have been accused of perpetuating the very wrongdoing they were meant to stop.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: Ji Chaoqun was a young engineer from Beijing heading to the US to study; then China’s spy agency came calling with bags of cash, the promise of glory and an offer that would change his life forever.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: A police raid in a small Philippine town reveals a young mayor's alleged links to a $2 billion Singapore money laundering scandal and Chinese espionage.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: A police raid in a small Philippine town reveals a young mayor's alleged ties to a $2 billion Singapore money laundering scandal and Chinese espionage.

programme.title: Bloomberg Investigates
programme.desc: How the son of a key adviser to Iran Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei built a secret oil empire.

programme.title: The Wall
programme.desc: An emotional documentary that will touch the heart of audiences around the world.; four female climbers face the sporting challenge of a lifetime as they attempt to compete in the first ever Olympic climbing competition at Tokyo 2020.

programme.title: Are Bots Killing Online Poker?
programme.desc: Computers have outplayed humans in chess and Go for decades. But poker was long considered unhackable-until bots from Russia and elsewhere got in the game.

programme.title: The Circuit
programme.desc: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, opens up in a wide-ranging interview with Emily Chang about life beyond the royal spotlight and her latest ventures in business and entertainment.

programme.title: The Circuit With Emily Chang
programme.desc: Emily Chang meets Snap CEO Evan Spiegel in Los Angeles for a conversation about his entrepreneurial journey, building Snap and his consumer hardware bet.

programme.title: The Circuit With Emily Chang
programme.desc: Emily Chang goes inside Stargate, a $500 billion bet on the future of AI; she speaks with OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and Softbank CEO Masayoshi Son about their hopes for the project.

programme.title: The Circuit
programme.desc: Emily Chang meets Reese Witherspoon and the Hello Sunshine executive team to discuss the company's founding mission and what's next for Hollywood.

programme.title: The Circuit With Emily Chang
programme.desc: Emily Chang meets Disney Entertainment Co-Chair Dana Walden for an inside look at the entertainment giant's global streaming and TV ambitions.

programme.title: The Circuit
programme.desc: Emily Chang meets Melinda French Gates in Seattle to discuss how she's navigating her next stage as a philanthropist and investor.

programme.title: The Future with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: Artificial intelligence has begun to exhibit early signs of agency.
